title: Custom Computer Programming Services

alternatives.name: Customer-specific application development firms
inclusion_criteria: More of the firm's service revenue is attributable to designing and developing bespoke software applications for particular customers than to any other enumerated archetype.
The firm owns delivery of application code or a defined development workstream, whether contracted as a project, dedicated team, or time-and-materials engagement.
The firm is estimated to fall in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA target band represented by the pinned dataset.
exclusion_criteria: Packaged-software customization or integration is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Website or database specialty work is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Temporary staffing, IT technical consulting, or software support is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
The establishment is primarily a software publisher, hosting provider, or computer-systems designer excluded from NAICS 541511 by the Census definition.
alternatives.rationale: The base is 5,767 target-band firms multiplied by a primary-equivalent share of 42.297%: the 2022 Economic Census reports that 61.3% of 541511 establishments provide custom software application design and development, and that this line supplies 69% of revenue at establishments reporting it. Multiplying prevalence by within-provider revenue share avoids counting every incidental service line as a whole firm. The 1,900-3,000 range allows for sampling variation, multiple service lines per establishment, firm-versus-establishment differences, and reweighting from the all-size Census population to the target band.

alternatives.name: Packaged-software customization and integration firms
inclusion_criteria: More of the firm's service revenue is attributable to customizing or integrating packaged software for customers than to any other enumerated archetype.
Customer work centers on configuring, extending, connecting, migrating, or implementing third-party or packaged applications.
The firm is estimated to fall in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA target band represented by the pinned dataset.
exclusion_criteria: Bespoke application development is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Website or database specialty work is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Temporary staffing, IT technical consulting, or software support is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
The firm primarily publishes its own software or primarily performs systems design without custom programming.
alternatives.rationale: The base is 5,767 multiplied by a 30.498% primary-equivalent share: 39.1% establishment prevalence for customization and integration of packaged software times a 78% within-provider revenue share. The range is broad because packaged integration and bespoke development commonly coexist, the product rows are not mutually exclusive, and the target-band mix may differ from the all-size industry mix. Its high endpoint overlaps the selected archetype's low endpoint, so it remains a plausible largest-count alternative.

alternatives.name: Website and database development specialists
inclusion_criteria: Website design and development or database design and development is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
The firm delivers customer-specific programming rather than primarily selling hosting, advertising, graphic design, or a published software product.
The firm is estimated to fall in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA target band represented by the pinned dataset.
exclusion_criteria: General bespoke application development is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Packaged-software customization or integration is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Temporary staffing, IT technical consulting, or software support is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Hosting, advertising, graphic design, or software publishing is the primary business.
alternatives.rationale: The base is 5,767 multiplied by 12.434%, combining primary-equivalent allocations for website services (30.8% prevalence times 31% within-provider revenue share) and database services (22.2% times 13%). The range reflects possible overlap between those product rows, the distinction between a specialty firm and an incidental line, and target-band reweighting.

alternatives.name: Programming capacity, technical consulting, and support-led firms
inclusion_criteria: Temporary staffing, IT technical consulting, or software-related technical support is the firm's largest service-revenue category while the establishment remains classified in NAICS 541511.
The customer primarily buys developer capacity, technical advice, or ongoing software support rather than vendor-owned delivery of a bespoke application or packaged-software implementation.
The firm is estimated to fall in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA target band represented by the pinned dataset.
exclusion_criteria: Bespoke application development is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Packaged-software customization or integration is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Website or database specialty work is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
The establishment is primarily an employment service, management consultancy, or managed IT provider classified outside NAICS 541511.
alternatives.rationale: The base is 5,767 multiplied by an 8.15% primary-equivalent share: temporary staffing (2.5% prevalence times 35% within-provider revenue share), IT technical consulting (11.6% times 25%), and IT technical support (17.5% times 25%). The range is wide because the rows may overlap, may be incidental at programming firms, and may include business models near other NAICS boundaries. The directory source confirms only that the engagement model exists; it does not estimate the count.

residual.name: Mixed, other, and unclassifiable 541511 target-band firms
residual.rationale: The base is the 6.621% remainder after allocating the four primary-equivalent archetypes against the 5,767-firm band estimate. It includes firms without a dominant enumerated line, firms led by smaller Census product categories, and classification ambiguity. The endpoints allow for overlap and nonresponse in the product-line bridge.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition and cross-references for 541511 Custom Computer Programming Services
2022 Economic Census data release and access page
2022 Economic Census Industry by Products Statistics archive
Clutch custom software development company directory and service taxonomy
Clutch U.S. IT staff augmentation company directory and service taxonomy

target_archetype.selection_basis: Stable archetype IDs were fixed before economic research. The pinned dataset estimates 5,767 target-band firms. For each Census product line, establishment prevalence was multiplied by that line's share of revenue at reporting establishments to form a primary-equivalent firm share, converting overlapping service-line presence into a principal-activity proxy. The base counts are 2,439 custom-application firms, 1,759 packaged-integration firms, 717 web/database specialists, and 470 capacity/consulting/support-led firms, plus a 382-firm residual. Custom application development wins the largest-base rule. Its coverage is 42.297% at base and approximately 32.9%-52.0% using its count range over the 5,767 denominator. Selection uncertainty is true because the selected range overlaps the packaged-integration range; this record remains solely about the selected archetype.

value_basis.scope: Source-free, class-neutral employee and controllable-contractor cash-cost taxonomy for target-band customer-specific application development firms. The weights allocate the combined employee and controllable-contractor cash-cost pool and do not express task removability, implementation, commercial capture, or any outcome.
value_basis.controllable_value_added_boundary: R_cva equals recognized revenue less only documented consideration contractually or economically owed to outside providers and not controlled as value added by the operator. No undocumented amount may be deducted, gross billed revenue is not a substitute, and a deducted cost may not also enter the V numerator.

roles.title: Employee software engineering and architecture
roles.rationale: Source-free bounded allocation for employee coding, technical design, code review, and engineering leadership in bespoke application delivery.

roles.title: Employee quality assurance and testing
roles.rationale: Source-free bounded allocation for employee test design, execution, defect triage, release checks, and quality ownership.

roles.title: Employee business analysis and delivery management
roles.rationale: Source-free bounded allocation for employee requirements discovery, customer translation, product coordination, and project delivery.

roles.title: Employee integration, platform, data, and delivery operations
roles.rationale: Source-free bounded allocation for employee integration, environments, deployment, platform operations, and data work; the category also spans firms near the packaged-integration boundary.

roles.title: Employee sales, solutions, and account management
roles.rationale: Source-free bounded allocation for employee business development, scoping support, proposals, contracting, and account stewardship.

roles.title: Employee corporate leadership and administration
roles.rationale: Source-free bounded allocation for employee executive, finance, people, legal, security, and general administrative work.

roles.title: Controllable-contractor software engineering and architecture
roles.rationale: Source-free bounded allocation for external engineers whose work product remains controlled value added of the operator and is not documented customer-bill pass-through.

roles.title: Controllable-contractor quality assurance and testing
roles.rationale: Source-free bounded allocation for external testing capacity controlled within the operator's delivery process and not treated as pass-through.

roles.title: Controllable-contractor business analysis and delivery management
roles.rationale: Source-free bounded allocation for external analysts and delivery specialists whose work remains part of the operator's controlled service.

roles.title: Controllable-contractor integration, platform, data, and delivery operations
roles.rationale: Source-free bounded allocation for external integration, platform, data, and operations specialists controlled by the operator.

roles.title: Controllable-contractor sales, solutions, and account support
roles.rationale: Source-free bounded allocation for external pre-sales or account support controlled by the operator.

roles.title: Controllable-contractor corporate and administrative support
roles.rationale: Source-free bounded allocation for external finance, people, legal, security, and administrative specialists whose services are controlled inputs rather than customer pass-through.

value_basis.rationale: The twelve mutually exclusive roles separate employee cash cost from controllable-contractor cash cost and sum to 1.00 across the combined pool. Employee roles sum to 0.75 and contractor roles to 0.25. Every weight is an ESTIMATE with NONE quality and no source; the structure is therefore an authoritative class-neutral assumption, not evidence. No pass-through category is pre-approved, and a contractor amount documented as third-party pass-through must be excluded from the contractor cash-cost identifier and deducted only from R_cva.
caveats: Assumption mode, ESTIMATE methods, NONE quality, and empty source lists force UNPROVEN readiness.
The point weights are bounded allocation assumptions required by the schema, not empirical confidence estimates.
Recognized revenue, every third-party pass-through category, positive bounded R_cva, and employee and contractor cash-cost amounts still require target-archetype evidence.
Do not assume zero pass-through, silently use gross billed revenue, or deduct the same subcontracted cost from both R_cva and the V numerator.
Selection uncertainty remains material because the selected and packaged-integration count ranges overlap.
The normalized dataset's broad labor share and 5415 role mix are not deterministic evidence for this target archetype.
No role weight encodes a removable fraction, implementation schedule, commercial retention, or expected outcome.

source_hints.source: Target-firm audited or reviewed financial statements, general-ledger detail, invoices, contracts, statements of work, subcontractor agreements, and revenue-recognition policies
source_hints.why: Needed to establish recognized revenue and separately document each economically owed third-party pass-through category so R_cva is positive, bounded, and free of double counting.

source_hints.source: Target-archetype payroll registers, benefits and burden schedules, contractor ledgers, delivery-team rosters, time records, and quality-of-earnings adjustments
source_hints.why: Needed to replace the source-free structure with bounded employee and controllable-contractor cash-cost amounts and target-specific role weights without importing pass-through costs.

source_hints.source: Target-archetype operator interviews, role-level workflow studies, implementation records, and customer delivery artifacts
source_hints.why: Needed to establish which frozen roles and tasks an evaluated intervention can affect, the adoption schedule, required controls, and realized rather than demonstrated performance.

source_hints.source: Customer contracts, pricing schedules, renewal records, win/loss data, and procurement interviews from target-band custom application firms
source_hints.why: Needed to distinguish fixed-price, time-and-materials, dedicated-team, and retainer mechanics and measure client repricing or competitive pass-through separately from accounting pass-through deducted from R_cva.

source_hints.source: Lower-middle-market transaction databases, broker processes, lender deal books, bidder logs, ownership records, and buyer interviews filtered to the selected archetype and EBITDA band
source_hints.why: Needed to estimate sale availability and, separately, the reference buyer's conditional sourcing, access, diligence, and win share at market-clearing price.

source_hints.source: Closed-transaction multiples, quality-of-earnings reports, lender underwriting, and downside exit evidence for custom application development firms
source_hints.why: Needed to support paid entry and downside exit multiples and test sensitivity to concentration, utilization, working capital, contractor reliance, and recurring-revenue mix.

source_hints.source: Customer procurement standards, liability and security requirements, contract accountability clauses, competitive entry data, and operator interviews
source_hints.why: Needed to determine whether an independent target-band operator retains a defensible delivery, trust, and accountability role through year five.

controllable_value_added: What recognized-revenue measure matches the target firm's accounting policy, period, and acquisition perimeter, with base, low, and high values?
Which subcontractor, cloud, license, hardware, travel, or other third-party amounts are contractually or economically owed outside the operator and separately documented as pass-through?
After excluding only documented pass-through, is R_cva positive, bounded, and internally consistent, and is every deducted amount excluded from the V cash-cost numerator?
Which contractor costs are controlled delivery inputs and which are third-party pass-through, with no overlap between treatments?
operational_realization: What target-archetype evidence supports employee cash compensation plus removable burden, controllable-contractor cash cost, and mutually exclusive role weights rather than broad-industry ratios?
For each evidenced role, which recurring tasks can the evaluated intervention technically affect, and what fraction of role cash cost do those tasks represent?
What adoption, integration, security, review, retraining, rework, and customer-acceptance steps are required in years one through five?
What target-archetype evidence distinguishes demonstrated capability from realized production use and sustained removable or avoidable cash cost?
commercial_retention: How are projects priced across fixed-price, time-and-materials, dedicated-team, and support arrangements in the selected archetype?
For each of years one through five, what share of operationally realized savings remains after client repricing, contractual sharing, renewals, rebids, rate-card changes, and competitive response?
How is commercial repricing kept separate from third-party accounting pass-through already removed from R_cva?
sale_availability: What share of independently owned selected-archetype firms in the target band becomes sale-available over five years?
How are broker coverage, unannounced transactions, add-ons, founder readiness, and duplicate listings bridged to a bounded five-year sale-availability share?
buyer_access_win: Conditional on sale availability, what share can the frozen reference buyer source, access, diligence, and win at prevailing market-clearing price?
How are process competition, geography, eligibility, diligence attrition, and win probability bridged without double counting or assuming privileged access?
valuation_robustness: What paid entry multiples are observed for selected-archetype firms in the target band, with explicit bridges for date, size, geography, and business model?
What downside exit multiple is supported for the normalized year-five state if utilization, growth, concentration, recurring-revenue mix, or competition deteriorates?
How sensitive are purchase economics to earnouts, working-capital adjustments, contractor normalization, founder replacement, and capitalized development practices?
operator_survival: Which customer-facing accountability, domain discovery, architecture, security, integration, and change-management functions must remain with a viable operator at year five?
Could customers, software platforms, independent specialists, staffing vendors, or new entrants bypass the selected operator, and on what supported timetable?
What evidence supports a bounded constant-price year-five survival probability for target-band customer-specific application development firms rather than the whole NAICS?

special_notes: This is an outcome-blind, class-neutral specification. Archetype IDs, counts, selection, R_cva treatment, and value-basis structure were fixed without consulting prompts, packets, scores, actions, or expected results.
The 5,767-firm band denominator is LOW-quality in the normalized dataset because it is inferred from employment-size classes and a broad margin proxy.

Selection uncertainty is true because the custom-application and packaged-integration count ranges overlap. The single primary record remains tied to customer-specific application development and is not a NAICS-wide claim.

The Economic Census table reports overlapping product-line presence across all establishment sizes. Prevalence times within-provider line share is a transparent primary-equivalent proxy, not a direct target-band firm count.

R_cva is MISSING until recognized revenue and every deducted third-party pass-through category have supported base, low, and high values. Undocumented pass-through may not be deducted, and gross billed revenue is not a silent substitute.

The v4.2 value basis is an authoritative source-free assumption: distinct employee and controllable-contractor identifiers and twelve ESTIMATE/NONE roles sum to 1.00, while cash-cost amounts, their ratio to R_cva, and all removable fractions require separate evidence.
Do not replace the frozen basis with the normalized dataset's broader 5415 role mix or all-NAICS labor share, and never count the same third-party cost in both the R_cva deduction and V numerator.
